title: arctotis × regalis
intro: Arctotis × regalis, also known as the royal African daisy, is a hybrid plant species within the Asteraceae family, derived from the native South African species Arctotis fastuosa and Arctotis adansonii. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits associated with Arctotis × regalis include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its extracts are also used in cosmetics and skincare products due to its purported skin-soothing and antioxidant effects.
